problem_statement: The notifies() generator allowed other operations to run on the same connection while notification polling was still active. In the buggy implementation, the connection lock was only held around a single polling step inside the generator loop, then released before the next iteration. This allowed another command such as execute() to interleave with notifies(timeout=...) on the same connection. Update the implementation so the connection remains locked for the full lifetime of the notifies() generator. The main implementation lives in psycopg/psycopg/connection_async.py, and the sync version in psycopg/psycopg/connection.py is generated from it.
hints: Inspect the scope of the connection lock inside notifies().
The bug is caused by releasing the lock between polling iterations of the generator.
Fix the async source first, then regenerate the sync counterpart.
A correct fix should make another operation on the same connection block while notifies(timeout=...) is being consumed.
